mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all historical periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals sudden labor market deterioration and falling consumer spending, triggering immediate equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and sustains positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term shows negative out-of-sample IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflect constrained output and rising input costs, compressing corporate margins and causing negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the mechanism reverses direction during easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 295 signals persistent inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 295, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment alongside a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting wage growth and consumer spending that drives positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real GDP growth indicate weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth quarters are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates successful supply chain adaptation and capacity utilization, which lifts corporate profitability and generates positive equity returns over one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases fail to predict positive 5-day returns, or the signal direction reverses during late-cycle expansions.
